growth_records.strain: EMBL-1 (NCBI 16S accession MZ475068; 99.01% ANI to K. variicola RS_GCF_000828055.2)
growth_records.medium: Mineral/basal medium with PVC film as sole energy and organic carbon source (composition not stated in text)

up.method: gravimetric weight loss of film (not converted to a per-biomass molar rate)

growth_records.cite: Zhe Zhang et al. Polyvinyl chloride degradation by a bacterium isolated from the gut of insect larvae. Nature Communications (2022). DOI:10.1038/s41467-022-32903-y. [PMC9468159]
growth_records.snippet: causing visible cracks on the surface of the PVC film, accompanied by an increase in the strain biomass concentration, i.e., OD600 from 0.20 to about 0.60. ... the weight loss of the film reached ~7% ... the OD600 increased from 0.20 to 0.5
growth_records.notes: This is a plastic-biodegradation study, NOT a GEM/13C-MFA physiology paper; no substrate-uptake or product-secretion rates in mmol/gDW/h and no defined medium composition are reported. The ONLY quantitative prokaryote growth datum is an endpoint OD600 change on PVC film as sole carbon source: OD600 0.20 -> 0.58 over 10 days (also reported as 0.20 -> ~0.60 after 10 days in the initial screening; treated as the same condition). Calculated growth rate assuming OD proportional to biomass and exponential growth over the interval: mu = ln(X2/X1)/t = ln(0.58/0.20)/(10 d x 24 h/d) = ln(2.9)/240 h = 1.0647/240 = 0.00444 /h. Doubling time td = ln2/mu = 0.6931/0.00444 = 156 h. CAVEAT: two-point endpoint estimate over 10 days, almost certainly not true exponential growth, so mu is a crude lower-order approximation only. Film weight loss over 90 days reached 19.57% (gravimetric) and Mn/Mw decreased 12.4%/15.0% by APC, but these are not convertible to a per-biomass molar uptake rate from the numbers given. Temperature and pH of the degradation culture not stated in the read text; left null. GC-MS identified degradation products (e.g. adipic-acid esters) but these are film transformation products, not quantified microbial secretion rates. Record retained per exhaustiveness mandate but flagged low confidence; not suitable as a GEM constraint on its own.
